notes: Statistics term for the 'tailedness' of a distribution, from Greek ќЇѕЕѕБѕДѕМѕВ (kurtos, 'curved/arching'); coined by Karl Pearson c.1905. Wiktionary GenAm /k…ЪЋИto Кs…™s/ вАФ primary stress on the second/middle syllable -TOH-, with a long 'oh'. Data scientists frequently flatten it to KUR-toh-sis (stress on first) or say ker-TAH-sis / ker-TOSS-is with a short 'o'. The leading 'ku-' is a plain 'ker-' schwa+r, not 'koor-'. Pairs with 'skewness' in every pandas .describe() conversation, so it's said often and missed often.
